title: America Reflects — And Immediately Needs Draining!
subtitle: The 250th-Birthday Pool Party Issue — BYO Algae
tagline: THE MAGAZINE THAT DRAINS THE SWAMP… THEN BILLS YOU $16 MILLION FOR THE WATER!

pages.alt: Trump in waders standing in the algae-green Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, holding a giant blue paint roller and a two-column Iran accord reading WHAT TRUMP SAID and WHAT IRAN SAYS, surrounded by Congress in tiny boats with a war-powers resolution, a chained HOUSING BILL houseboat, an $87.6 billion sandbag, a snorkeling IAEA inspector duck, and a New York subway car labeled MAMDANI EFFECT crashing into the corner.

coverLines: AMERICA REFLECTS — AND IMMEDIATELY NEEDS DRAINING!
WAR POWERS: NOW AVAILABLE IN SYMBOLIC SIZE
BIPARTISAN BILL USED AS HOSTAGE FURNITURE
PLUS: THE MAMDANI SUBWAY CAR DERAILS THE ESTABLISHMENT!

pages.title: FROM THE EDITOR'S BUNKER
paragraphs: We stood at the edge of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool this week, gazed into its $16-million algae-green surface, and asked the eternal question of our republic: who did this? The pool, performing the one job a reflecting pool has ever had, showed us the answer. We are not allowed to print the answer. The answer has retained counsel.

It has been a banner 36 hours. The Senate voted 50–48 to tell the President to stop a war — the first time in the 50-year history of the law that both chambers have agreed on anything this strongly, and also, we are told, a vote with roughly the legal force of a strongly-worded Yelp review. The President called the four Republicans who crossed over “Losers.” The Republicans called it “a briefing they never got.” The war continued to cost $87.6 billion regardless, which the White House would now like Congress to please pay for, along with some Ebola money and $11.1 billion for farmers, all in the same sandbag.

Meanwhile Iran agreed, according to the President, to “highest level” nuclear inspections — “Infinity!!!” Iran, according to Iran, agreed to no such thing. Both statements were issued on the same afternoon, from the same planet, about the same uranium. We have printed both, side by side, in a feature we are calling “The Accord That Disagrees With Itself.”

And in a rare outbreak of Congress doing its literal job, both chambers passed the largest housing bill in a generation — 85–5 and 358–32 — only for the President to cancel the signing ceremony two hours before it began, chaining his signature to a separate voter-ID bill like a houseboat lashed to a buoy. The housing bill becomes law in ten days whether he signs it or not, which is the legislative equivalent of refusing to blow out a candle that is going to go out anyway.

Down in New York, a subway car full of Mamdani-endorsed socialists crashed directly into the Democratic establishment and took two incumbents with it. We cover that too. We cover everything. We are exhausted. The pool is still green.
Drain responsibly. Happy 250th. BYO algae.
pages.signature: — The Editors, dabbing at their waders
pages.ps: P.S. The duck on the cover wearing the snorkel is a credentialed IAEA inspector. He has clearance for the shallow end only. The deep end is “pending a final deal and sanctions relief.”

staff.name: Algernon Q. Bloom, fished out of the pool

pages.scene: A bright Mad Magazine style cartoon of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool turned into a swamp: algae-green water, peeling 'American Flag Blue' paint flaking into the water, ducklings paddling in tiny hazmat safety goggles. A large lopsided wooden sign at the water's edge reads '350-FOOT GASH — EVIDENCE PENDING'. A bewildered National Park Service ranger wades out holding a net, weeping. Exaggerated expressions, crowded comic chaos, the Lincoln Memorial columns in the background.
pages.heading: THE POOL THAT REFLECTS EVERYTHING
pages.subhead: A $16 Million Renovation, A 350-Foot “Gash,” And An Algae That Has Gone Undefeated Since 1922
pages.byline: By Our Drainage Correspondent, filing from a kayak
paragraphs: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool was renovated for the nation's 250th birthday at a cost of $16 million, and in return for that sum the American people received a pool that is, at press time, the color of a smoothie made entirely of regret. The water is algae-green. The new paint — a shade officially logged as “American Flag Blue” — is peeling into the water in patriotic flakes. Ducklings paddle through it wearing what our photographer swears are tiny safety goggles.

Democrats are demanding investigations. The President has blamed vandals, citing a claimed “350-foot gash” in the pool liner, for which — and we want to be precise here — no public evidence has yet been produced. The sign at the water's edge reads “350-FOOT GASH — EVIDENCE PENDING,” which is also, increasingly, the official motto of the Republic.

“Someone did this,” a source close to the pool told us, gesturing at the pool, which was at that moment reflecting the source perfectly. “A bad someone. A vandal someone. Definitely not anyone who signed the invoice.” When we pointed out that the reflection appeared to be pointing back at the source, the source said the reflection was “fake news water” and left.

Contractors and the White House defend the work, noting that the pool is, technically, still a pool, and that algae is a “naturally occurring patriot” that “loves this country.” The National Park Service was unavailable, having last been seen wading out toward the center holding a net and weeping softly into it.
INSANE reached out to the algae for comment. The algae, which has been winning continuously since the pool opened in 1922, declined, citing momentum.
pages.pullQuote: The pool was asked who did this. Being a reflecting pool, it answered. That was the whole problem.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of a politician in waders standing in a green swampy reflecting pool, triumphantly holding up a giant open two-column document. The left column is headed 'WHAT TRUMP SAID' and the right column 'WHAT IRAN SAYS', the two columns visibly contradicting each other with little angry scribbles between them. A snorkeling inspector duck in goggles bobs nearby next to an underwater barrel labeled 'URANIUM'. Bright comic colors, exaggerated satirical expressions.
pages.heading: THE ACCORD THAT DISAGREES WITH ITSELF
pages.subhead: Trump Says Iran Agreed To “Highest Level” Inspections — “Infinity!!!” Iran Says It Agreed To Nothing Of The Sort. Both Were Said On The Same Day.
pages.byline: By Our Iran Accord Translator (he translates it into itself)
paragraphs: In one of the great achievements of modern diplomacy, the United States and Iran this week reached an accord that the two parties describe completely differently while standing in the same room. The President holds up a document with two columns. The left column is headed WHAT TRUMP SAID. The right column is headed WHAT IRAN SAYS. The columns do not match. They were never going to match. Matching was not in the framework.

Per the President, Iran agreed to “highest level” nuclear inspections, “anywhere, anytime,” “Infinity!!!” — a unit of inspection that, our science desk confirms, does not come with batteries and cannot be independently verified, much like infinity itself.

Per Iran, Iran has agreed to no “Infinity.” Iranian officials say inspectors may return under an interim framework, but that access to the facilities the U.S. recently bombed — the interesting ones, with the underwater uranium barrels our cover artist so lovingly rendered — depends on a final deal and on sanctions relief. “Not so fast,” the right column reads. “We will decide.”

The IAEA, caught in the middle wearing a snorkel, confirms that some inspections will happen under the interim framework, and that the rest will happen approximately when everyone agrees on what they agreed to. Our IAEA inspector duck has been cleared for the shallow end. He reports the shallow end is fine. The deep end remains “pending.”
We ran both columns past a neutral arbitrator. The arbitrator read the left column, read the right column, read them again, and is now also a reflecting pool.
pages.pullQuote: “Infinity!!!” said the left column. “We will decide,” said the right column. They are the same document. This is the document.

terms.def: An altitude. Inspections will be conducted from it, looking down, with a telescope, optionally.
Anywhere we already inspected; anytime that is not now.
A quantity of inspections equal to itself plus one, forever, sold separately, batteries not included.
Official Iranian unit of access, convertible to the shallow end only, pending sanctions relief.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of tiny senators in a small wooden rowboat floating on an algae-green reflecting pool, paddling with oars, holding up an oversized soggy scroll labeled 'WAR POWERS RESOLUTION 50-48'. The scroll is comically tiny and stamped 'SYMBOLIC SIZE'. Two empty senator seats labeled with question marks. The boat goes nowhere. Crowded comic chaos, exaggerated worried faces.
pages.heading: WAR POWERS: NOW AVAILABLE IN SYMBOLIC SIZE
pages.subhead: The Senate Voted 50–48 To Direct The President To Halt The War. The President Is Directed To, And May Now Ignore It, As Directed.
pages.byline: By Our War Powers Desk, floating in a tiny boat
paragraphs: For the first time in the 50-year life of the War Powers law, both chambers of Congress have passed a resolution directing the President to remove U.S. forces from hostilities — this one with Iran. The Senate vote was 50–48. It was the tenth attempt. It was, by every account, a stunning turnaround. It is also, by every account including its own, largely symbolic and may carry the legal force of a firmly-folded paper boat.

Four Republicans crossed over: Cassidy, Murkowski, Collins, and Paul. One Democrat, Fetterman, voted against. Two Republicans, McConnell and McCormick, did not vote at all — and had they shown up and voted no, the whole thing would have tied 50–50 and sunk. The resolution thus passed because two of its likely opponents were elsewhere, which is the most honest thing that has happened in the Capitol all year.

Senator Cassidy explained his vote by saying the administration had never actually told Americans what was going on, and that he would keep voting for war powers until somebody briefed him. This is a man asking, repeatedly and on the record, to please be told what the war is. He has not been told. He keeps voting. The war keeps going. The briefing remains, like the gash, pending.

The President responded by calling the four crossover Republicans “Losers” who had “made my job more difficult,” and described the resolution as “poorly timed and meaningless.” The resolution, for its part, was introduced as a concurrent resolution, meaning it does not go to the President's desk for a signature, meaning he cannot veto it, meaning everyone is now yelling about a document that has no inbox.
Our War Powers Desk, paddling its tiny boat across the Reflecting Pool, reports that the resolution floats beautifully, directs the current admirably, and changes the current not at all.
pages.pullQuote: It passed because two senators who'd have killed it weren't there. It directs the President to do a thing. The thing is optional. Welcome to war powers in symbolic size.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of a wooden houseboat labeled 'BIPARTISAN HOUSING BILL' floating on a green swampy pool, chained with a heavy padlock to a red buoy labeled 'SAVE ACT FIRST'. The houseboat strains against the chain trying to float away. A giant disembodied hand dangles a signing pen just out of reach. Bright satirical comic style, exaggerated tension, algae everywhere.
pages.heading: THE HOSTAGE HOUSEBOAT
pages.subhead: Congress Passed The Biggest Housing Bill In A Generation. The President Canceled The Signing Two Hours Early And Chained His Pen To A Voter-ID Bill.
pages.byline: By Our Housing Bill Hostage Negotiator
paragraphs: In a near-miracle, both chambers of Congress passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act — 85–5 in the Senate, 358–32 in the House — the most significant housing legislation since 1990. It speeds construction and, notably, limits big institutional investors from buying up single-family homes. It was a rare, gleaming, fully-bipartisan thing, bobbing in the pool like a houseboat that actually works.

Two hours before the scheduled signing ceremony at the Capitol, the President canceled it. He will not sign the housing bill, he announced, until Congress passes the separate SAVE America Act, a proof-of-citizenship voter-ID measure he has declared a “National Emergency.” In our cover illustration this is depicted accurately: the houseboat is labeled HOUSING BILL and chained to a buoy labeled SAVE ACT FIRST. We changed nothing. That's the news.

Here is the part that makes the hostage situation especially philosophical: under the Constitution, a bill that has passed both chambers becomes law in ten days whether the President signs it or not, as long as Congress stays in session — and this one passed with veto-proof majorities besides. The President is, in effect, refusing to blow out a candle that is going out on its own, while announcing that the candle is his hostage.

Senator Warren, a lead author, noted that huge bipartisan majorities passed a bill to lower housing costs and that the President is refusing, at the eleventh hour, to sign it. Speaker Johnson supported the refusal while also confirming the President will probably sign it within ten days anyway, which is the political equivalent of standing very firm in a doorway that has no door.
INSANE attempted to interview the houseboat. It declined, citing the chain.
pages.pullQuote: It becomes law in ten days whether he signs it or not. He has taken hostage a bill that escapes on its own. This is the strongest possible position to negotiate from, if your goal is a press release.

ad.headline: WHY PASS A BILL WHEN YOU CAN CHAIN IT TO A DIFFERENT BILL?
bullets: 🔗 Every purchase chained to a SECOND, unrelated purchase!
🏠 Houseboat sold separately and also held hostage!
📋 Includes one (1) National Emergency of your choosing!
⏳ Becomes yours in 10 days whether you want it or not!
ad.price: FREE WITH ANY VOTER-ID PURCHASE (THE BILL WAS ALREADY FREE)
ad.disclaimer: Hostage may free itself constitutionally. Furniture not responsible for candles that go out on their own. Chain is symbolic, like everything else this week.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon montage of ordinary American citizens of various ages standing at the edge of an algae-green reflecting pool, leaning over and squinting into the water, each with a comic speech bubble and an exaggerated baffled expression. One holds a measuring tape. A duckling in hazmat goggles paddles toward the deep end. Crowded vibrant comic panel energy.
pages.heading: MAN & WOMAN IN THE STREET
pages.subhead: We Asked Real Americans To Gaze Into The Reflecting Pool And Tell Us What They Saw
paragraphs: DALE HORNBECK, 54, surveyor, Tysons Corner, VA: “I measured the gash myself. It's not 350 feet. It's not 3 feet. There is no gash. There's a pool, and it's green, and a man is standing in it with a paint roller. I'm a surveyor. I survey. That's my survey.”

PRISCILLA VANCE-OKONKWO, 39, accountant, Silver Spring, MD: “Sixteen million dollars. For context, you could repaint this pool eight thousand times at Home Depot prices, and at least one of those times it would stay blue. I did the math. The math is also green now. Everything I touch turns green.”

BUTCH KRZYZEWSKI, 61, retired, Parma, OH: “War powers, my foot. They voted fifty to forty-eight to tell the man to stop, and the man said no, and that was that. You know what's got more force of law than that resolution? My ex-wife's note on the fridge. At least I'm scared of that one.”

SUNNY DELACROIX-PFEIFFER, 27, barista, Columbus, OH: “The President says Iran agreed to infinity inspections and Iran says they didn't agree to anything and both of them said it Tuesday. I serve eleven-dollar oat lattes to people who are calmer than this. And those people are NOT calm.”

WENDELL ABERNATHY-GROSS, 72, war powers enthusiast, Manassas, VA: “I've waited fifty years for both chambers to pass a war powers resolution. FIFTY YEARS. And it finally happens, and it turns out it's the symbolic kind. It's like waiting your whole life to catch a fish and the fish is a folded-up piece of paper that says FISH.”
DUCKLING #4, 2 weeks, Reflecting Pool, DC: “[Wearing hazmat goggles. Declined to comment. Paddled toward the deep end the IAEA has not been cleared to inspect. We worry about Duckling #4.]”
pages.pullQuote: “It's like waiting your whole life to catch a fish and the fish is a folded-up piece of paper that says FISH.”

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of an enormous burlap sandbag labeled '$87.6 BILLION' splashing into an algae-green reflecting pool, sending green water flying. The bag is stamped with an itemized receipt reading 'WAR $67.15B • EBOLA RESPONSE • FARMERS $11.1B • BLAME MANAGEMENT $50,000'. Tiny ducklings scatter from the splash wearing goggles. Exaggerated comic motion, water droplets, satirical chaos.
pages.heading: THE $87.6 BILLION SANDBAG
pages.subhead: The White House Asks Congress For A Supplemental So Large It Has Its Own Splash. War Money, Ebola Money, And Farmer Money, All In One Bag.
paragraphs: Into the Reflecting Pool, with a splash heard across the Mall, the White House has lobbed a request for $87.6 billion in supplemental funding. The bag is itemized on the side like a heroic grocery receipt: roughly $67.15 billion for military needs tied largely to the Iran war, plus money for the Ebola response, plus $11.1 billion for farmers, all bundled together so that voting against the war means voting against the farmers and also, somehow, against not getting Ebola.

This is a classic of the genre: the omnibus splash. You cannot remove the war money without getting Ebola on your hands and dropping the farmers in the pool. The bag is designed to be un-untieable. Our Splash Correspondent attempted to untie it and is now, himself, partially Ebola response.

The timing is exquisite. Days after the Senate voted 50–48 that the war should stop, the administration has asked Congress to please fund the war it just voted to stop, which Congress will likely do, because the alternative is explaining to a farmer why his $11.1 billion was attached to a war the funder of which has been directed, symbolically, to end.
The sandbag now sits in the shallow end, leaking $50,000 of “blame management” into the surrounding algae. The ducklings have unionized.
pages.pullQuote: You cannot vote against the war money without dropping the farmers in the pool. That is not a bug in the sandbag. That is the sandbag.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of gasoline pumps bobbing like buoys in an algae-green reflecting pool, their hoses dangling into the green water. Two pumps wear nervous cartoon faces, labeled 'EXXON' and 'CHEVRON'. A stern DOJ official in a suit peers at them through a magnifying glass from the shore. A crude-oil price arrow rockets up and floats down like a feather. Bright satirical comic chaos.
pages.heading: PUMP BUOYS & THE GREAT OIL PROBE
pages.subhead: Gas Pumps Bob In The Pool Like Buoys. The President Has Ordered The DOJ To Ask Exxon And Chevron Why The Price Isn't Falling Faster.
paragraphs: Crude prices dropped. The price at the pump did not drop as fast. In response, the President ordered the Department of Justice to take a look at the oil companies — later naming Exxon and Chevron specifically — over allegations that they are not lowering pump prices quickly enough to suit the moment.

On our cover, the gas pumps bob in the Reflecting Pool like buoys, which is the most accurate possible depiction of American energy policy: floating, bobbing, technically present, going wherever the current goes, dispensing fuel into an algae bloom.

The oil companies, for their part, note that they are large, that prices are complicated, and that crude does not become a gallon of regular unleaded merely because a President has gestured at it on a Tuesday. The DOJ noted that it would “take a look,” which is what the DOJ says about everything, and then took a look, in the way that one looks at a pool one has been told contains a 350-foot gash one cannot see.

INSANE's economists confirm prices are sticky on the way down. This is called “rockets and feathers”: prices rise like rockets and fall like feathers. The feathers, in this case, are floating in the pool, next to the pumps, next to the ducklings, next to the algae, which is winning.
pages.pullQuote: Crude fell like a rocket. The pump price is falling like a feather. The feather is in the pool. Everything is in the pool.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of a New York City subway car bursting out of an algae-green reflecting pool at an angle, crashing through the corner, labeled 'MAMDANI EFFECT' on the side and 'INCUMBENTS OUT' on the front. Three cheering progressive candidates wave from the windows while two stunned establishment politicians tumble out the back doors into the green water. Exaggerated comic motion, water spray, vibrant energy.
pages.heading: THE MAMDANI SUBWAY CAR DERAILS THE ESTABLISHMENT
pages.subhead: Three Mamdani-Backed Candidates — Valdez, Lander, Avila Chevalier — Win Democratic Primaries, Taking Two Incumbents Down With The Train
pages.byline: By Our NYC Bureau, riding the 3rd rail
paragraphs: Up from New York, crashing into the corner of our cover like a runaway train, comes the night the Democratic establishment got derailed. Three congressional candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their primaries on Tuesday — and two of them did it by ousting sitting incumbents.

Former City Comptroller Brad Lander defeated Rep. Dan Goldman handily, around 66% to 34%, in the 10th District. Community organizer Darializa Avila Chevalier edged out five-term Rep. Adriano Espaillat by roughly three points in the 13th — one of the biggest New York House upsets since a young bartender named Ocasio-Cortez beat Joe Crowley back in 2018. And State Assemblymember Claire Valdez took the open 7th District seat outright.

House Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries campaigned aggressively against the Mamdani slate. The Mamdani slate won anyway. Mamdani called the trio “our team,” having filmed an ad with them that aired after Game 1 of the NBA Finals in which he passed a basketball back and forth with his three future congressmen. This is, we note, a more functional handoff than anything happening at the Reflecting Pool.
The subway car on our cover bears the slogan MAMDANI EFFECT and the words INCUMBENTS OUT. We did not editorialize. We drew the train. The train did the rest. The train always does.
pages.pullQuote: Jeffries campaigned against them. The train ran anyway. The train does not check the schedule with leadership.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon gallery wall of framed portraits of caricatured politicians at a podium, each with an enormous speech bubble, mouths open mid-proclamation, exaggerated confident grins. A red velvet 'HALL OF FAME' banner reads 'CRAZY COMMENTS'. One politician points at himself proudly. Bright satirical comic style, crowded gallery energy, ornate gold frames slightly crooked.
pages.heading: CRAZY COMMENTS!
pages.subheading: Real Quotes. Really Said. We Checked. We're As Surprised As You Are.
pages.deck: INSANE's Hall of Fame for the week's most baffling, math-defying, and self-contradicting statements — printed VERBATIM, because we could not possibly improve on them. Every quote below is real and accurately reproduced. The commentary is ours. The words are theirs.
pages.caption: ★ All quotes are genuine and on the record. We are not making these up. That is the entire point. ★
paragraphs:
🏆 THE “INFINITY!!!” AWARD FOR NUCLEAR CERTAINTY — President Donald Trump (Truth Social):
“Iran has fully and completely agreed to highest level Nuclear inspections long into the future (Infinity!!!). This will insure ‘Nuclear Honesty.’ If they did not agree to this, there would be no further negotiations!”
— INSANE notes: Iran's Foreign Ministry, the same day, said it had made “no new commitments” and had no plans to allow inspections of the attacked sites. Both statements are real. Both were said. Infinity remains, as ever, batteries not included.

📋 THE REBUTTAL DESK — Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei (Tehran news conference):
“We do not intend to allow IAEA inspectors to visit the nuclear sites that were attacked in the war.”
— INSANE notes: Printed here purely so you can hold it up next to the “Infinity!!!” post and watch the two of them refuse to be in the same room.

🌟 THE “MAJOR MILESTONE” FOR A THING THAT DIDN'T HAPPEN — Vice President JD Vance (Switzerland press conference):
“The Iranians have agreed to invite IAEA inspectors back into their country… That is a major milestone for the American people.”
— INSANE notes: The Iranians, contacted for comment by reality, declined to confirm they had agreed to the milestone they were a major part of.

⚖️ THE “PROVIDED AID AND COMFORT” PROCLAMATION (Spelling Preserved) — President Donald Trump (Truth Social):
“Senate decides to have a poorly timed and meaningless War Powers Act Vote, telling the Number One Sponser of Terror in the World… and by so doing has provided aid and comfort the Enemy.”
— INSANE notes: “Sponser” is reproduced exactly as posted. We have a copy editor. He wept. The Constitution's “aid and comfort” language refers to treason, which the Senate committed by voting, which is its job.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon split scene: on one side a politician at a gas station yanking a fuel-price sign downward shouting at it, prices comically falling like rocks and floating up like feathers; on the other side the same politician giving a thumbs-up next to a female senator with a giant gold star reading 'SANE', while a tiny calendar behind them flips to a page reading 'LOSER'. Bright satirical comic style, exaggerated contradictory expressions.
pages.heading: CRAZY COMMENTS, PART TWO!
pages.subheading: The Gas-Pump Physics & Self-Reversing-Endorsement Wing Of The Hall Of Fame
pages.deck: More genuine, verbatim, on-the-record statements — this time on the subjects of petroleum, loyalty, and the binding force of documents nobody has to sign.
pages.caption: ★ Still real. Still verbatim. Still happening. ★
paragraphs:
🪨 THE GEOLOGY-DEFYING GAS-PUMP THEOREM — President Donald Trump (Truth Social):
“The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! …customers are being ‘gouged.’ Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I'm seeing!”
— INSANE notes: Filed with the Department of Justice as an official “look” at Exxon and Chevron. Rocks, for the record, fall. Pump prices, for the record, feather. This is the entire field of energy economics, and it is now a DOJ matter.

🔁 THE SELF-REVERSING ENDORSEMENT — President Donald Trump, on Sen. Susan Collins, EARLIER this month:
“She is a sane woman.”
— INSANE notes: Days later, after Collins voted for the war-powers resolution, the same president grouped her among “Four Republican Losers.” The sane woman and the Republican loser are, we are reliably informed, the same Maine senator, observed at two different moments of presidential favor.

💪 THE “I ALWAYS GET IT DONE” GUARANTEE — President Donald Trump (Truth Social):
“These Senators have just made my job more difficult, but I will get it done, one way or the other, because I always get it done!”
— INSANE notes: The job in question — ending or authorizing the war — is the one the Senate just voted 50–48 to make him do. He is now vowing to get done the thing he is mad at them for telling him to do.

📜 THE “ACTUALLY, IT'S BINDING” COUNTERPOINT — Rep. Gregory Meeks (statement after Senate passage):
“Regardless of what President Trump says, this measure is binding under the War Powers Resolution.”
— INSANE notes: Included in the interest of balance, and because somebody in this issue should sound like they've read the law. The courts will now decide whether the paper boat floats. We await the ruling from a kayak.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine 'Spy vs Spy' style cartoon, black-and-white pointy-nosed spies (one in black, one in white) battling over a rubber drain plug at the bottom of an algae-green reflecting pool. The white spy is disguised with a snorkel and an 'IAEA INSPECTOR' badge. A sign reads '350-FOOT GASH — EVIDENCE PENDING'. Classic crosshatch Spy vs Spy comic style, slapstick sabotage energy.
pages.title: SPY vs. SPY
pages.subtitle: “The Drain Plug” — Two Agents, One Rubber Stopper, Infinite Swamp

panels: WHITE SPY, disguised as a credentialed IAEA inspector duck, snorkels to the bottom of the Reflecting Pool and locates the master drain plug. Today, he drains the swamp.
He swaps BLACK SPY's plug for an identical one stamped “INFINITY!!! — BATTERIES NOT INCLUDED.” Black Spy yanks it triumphantly. Nothing drains. The plug was purely symbolic.
Smugly, WHITE SPY notes the pool drains on its own in ten days regardless — constitutionally. He gloats. He accepts a handed-over $16 million invoice on reflex. He should not have.
BLACK SPY finds the master drain plug first, hidden beneath a sign reading “350-FOOT GASH — EVIDENCE PENDING.” He grins. The credit will be his.
Foiled by the symbolic plug, BLACK SPY chains the REAL plug to a buoy labeled “SAVE ACT FIRST,” taking the drain itself hostage until his unrelated demands are met.
As WHITE SPY gloats, BLACK SPY quietly hands him the renovation invoice. White Spy is now financially responsible for the entire pool. Checkmate.

pages.result: The pool, reflecting both spies perfectly, drains at last — revealing a single $16 million invoice and the algae, still winning, now also fully briefed on the war, which is more than Senator Cassidy can say.

pages.scene: A Mad Magazine style cartoon of a chaotic waterlogged classified-ads bulletin board half-submerged in an algae-green reflecting pool, job listings pinned everywhere and floating on the water, a carnival barker in a suit shouting 'HELP WANTED' from a rowboat. Applicants include a snorkeling inspector duck, a pool contractor with a dripping paint roller, and a glum politician holding a 'WILL LEGISLATE FOR A SEAT' sign. Crowded comic job-fair chaos.
pages.heading: INSANE CLASSIFIEDS!
pages.subheading: JOBS! OPPORTUNITIES! POSITIONS BOBBING IN THE POOL! (READ WHILE TREADING WATER.)
pages.deck: This Week's Employment Listings, Personals, And Opportunities From The INSANE Marketplace — Now Soggy — We Are Not Responsible For The Applicants These Ads Will Attract Or What The Water Reflects Back At Them
pages.caption: ★ INSANE Magazine assumes no liability for the positions below. All listings are satire. Do not actually wade in. ★
paragraphs:
POOL CONTRACTOR — LINCOLN MEMORIAL, WASHINGTON DC
Prestigious federal monument seeks coatings contractor for high-visibility renovation. Prior experience: one (1) decorative pond preferred but not required. Must be comfortable painting a surface “American Flag Blue” and having it turn algae-green within fourteen (14) days. Estimate of $1.8 million will be honored as a starting point only; final invoice flexible up to and including $16 million. No-bid, no-fuss, no-idea. Knowledge of a “350-foot gash” a plus — gash need not actually exist. Apply: leave résumé floating, surface-side up.

IAEA NUCLEAR INSPECTOR — INTERIM FRAMEWORK, SHALLOW END ONLY
International body seeks credentialed inspector for “highest level” inspections, “Infinity!!!”, batteries not included. Snorkel provided. Clearance for shallow end only; deep end access pending a final deal and sanctions relief. Must be comfortable being described by one party as having full access and by the other party as having no agreement whatsoever, simultaneously, in the same room. Duck applicants encouraged. Apply: tread water near the underwater uranium barrels and wait.

WAR POWERS RESOLUTION — SYMBOLIC SIZE, IMMEDIATE OPENING
Historic document seeks meaning. Passed both chambers of Congress for the first time in 50 years; now seeking any legal force whatsoever. Floats beautifully. Directs the President to do a thing he is fully entitled to ignore. Ideal candidate: a court willing to take this seriously. Compensation: the satisfaction of a job nominally done. Apply: introduce as a concurrent resolution so no one has to sign anything.

HOUSING BILL — SEEKS SIGNATURE, WILL WAIT 10 DAYS REGARDLESS
Largest housing bill in a generation, passed 85–5 and 358–32, seeks one (1) presidential signature. Currently chained to an unrelated voter-ID buoy and held hostage. Note: becomes law in ten days whether signed or not, so this is less a job and more a formality we are all performing together. Veto-proof and emotionally available. Apply: the Constitution will handle it; you needn't do anything.

OIL EXECUTIVE — MUST EXPLAIN ROCKETS & FEATHERS
Major energy firm (initials E.X. or C.H.) seeks spokesperson to explain why pump prices rise like rockets and fall like feathers. Must withstand a DOJ “look.” Comfortable bobbing buoy-like in a reflecting pool while dispensing fuel into an algae bloom. Crude-to-gallon conversion knowledge helpful; ability to do it on a President's Tuesday timeline: impossible, but apply anyway. Apply: the pump is floating; flag it down.

PERSONALS — DEMOCRATIC INCUMBENT, NY, SEEKS SEAT
Recently un-renominated five-term congressman, warm, experienced, fixture of the community, seeks any congressional district not currently being run over by a subway car labeled MAMDANI EFFECT. Must love affordable housing and merengue. No socialists, please — have met enough this week. Dan and Adriano need not reply to each other. Apply: concede gracefully around 10:30 p.m.

pages.title: 🏆 THE INSANE AWARDS
pages.date: Honoring Excellence In Deniable Infrastructure & Symbolic Governance

categories.name: MOST LOAD-BEARING PIECE OF EVIDENCE NEVER PRODUCED
categories.gold: The 350-Foot Gash — so large it explains a $16M algae bloom, so invisible it has never been photographed. The single most powerful object in government, precisely because it may not exist.
categories.silver: The vandals who allegedly cut it — named by no one, seen by no one, blamed by everyone.
categories.bronze: The pending evidence itself, which remains, like the war briefing Senator Cassidy keeps requesting, pending.

categories.name: BEST USE OF THE WORD “INFINITY!!!”
categories.gold: As a unit of nuclear inspection Iran says it never agreed to — batteries not included, cannot be verified.
categories.silver: As three exclamation points doing the work of an entire arms-control framework.
categories.bronze: As an answer to “how many times will you say a deal exists while the other party denies it?”

categories.name: MOST SYMBOLIC ACT OF GOVERNANCE
categories.gold: The 50–48 war-powers resolution — passed for the first time in 50 years, on two opponents being absent, directing the President to do a thing he can ignore.
categories.silver: Taking a housing bill hostage that becomes law in ten days whether you sign it or not.
categories.bronze: A DOJ “look” at oil companies for not lowering prices on a Tuesday timeline.

pages.scene: A wide bright Mad Magazine style fold-in illustration of the algae-green Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, with a politician in waders holding a paint roller, senators in tiny boats, a chained housing houseboat, an $87.6 billion sandbag, a snorkeling inspector duck, and a crowd on the bank all pointing in every direction shouting 'WHO DID THIS?'. Designed so that when the left third folds over to meet the right, the pool surface becomes a mirror and the combined reflection points back at the figure in the waders. Bright comic style engineered for the fold.
pages.heading: THE INSANE FOLD-IN
pages.instruction: FOLD PAGE OVER LIKE THIS — A OVER B — TO SEE WHAT THE POOL REFLECTS
pages.alt: A wide Mad-Magazine-style illustration of the drained-and-refilled Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, algae-green, with the President in waders and paint roller, Congress in tiny boats, the chained housing houseboat, the $87.6 billion sandbag, the snorkeling IAEA duck, and a crowd on the bank all pointing in every direction shouting WHO DID THIS? When the left third folds over to meet the right, the surface becomes a single mirror and the combined reflection points squarely back at the figure in the waders. Bright comic style, engineered for the fold.
pages.question: WE DRAINED THE POOL, INVESTIGATED THE GASH, AND ASKED, ON BEHALF OF A GRATEFUL NATION, WHO IS TRULY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE STATE OF THIS REFLECTING POOL?
pages.answer: THE ONE HOLDING THE ROLLER. THE POOL KNEW. IT IS A REFLECTING POOL. REFLECTING IS THE ONLY JOB IT HAS EVER HAD.
pages.rightText: ← FOLD LEFT SIDE OVER TO MEET THE REFLECTION
